demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; approximately 30 minutes from the nearest tertiary hospital

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I feel incredibly weak, my teeth will not stop chattering from the cold, and my gums just started bleeding for no reason.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My spouse called because I had chemotherapy about ten days ago and tonight I suddenly spiked a high fever with violent shaking chills, I'm completely wiped out, and now my gums are oozing blood and I've had a nosebleed.

symptom_narrative.onset: extreme fatigue and weakness started yesterday, about ten days after my last chemotherapy; two hours ago I got sudden violent shaking chills and a high fever
symptom_narrative.duration: the fatigue for about a day; the fever, rigors and bleeding for the last couple of hours
symptom_narrative.character: uncontrollable shaking and teeth-chattering from the cold, a burning high fever, drenching sweats, and now blood slowly oozing from my gums with a nosebleed
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I have felt during the whole treatment — I can't get up and I'm shaking too hard to hold a cup
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: all over — feverish and shivering everywhere, with the bleeding from my gums and nose
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: came on suddenly two hours ago and has kept getting worse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — I took paracetamol two hours ago and it did nothing to the fever
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building on its own no matter what I do
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: drenching sweats, feeling faint and lightheaded when I try to sit up, and some new little blood spots and bruises on my forearms
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no — I have not had a fever like this before during treatment
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed and completely dependent on my spouse; I can't stand or manage fluids on my own

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: cancer that I am being treated for with chemotherapy
past_medical_history.how_managed: systemic chemotherapy; last cycle about ten days ago

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: chemotherapy (last cycle about ten days ago)

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: 1000 mg taken about two hours ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and making the call; about 30 minutes from the nearest tertiary hospital and worried about getting there safely

objective_data_offered.value: 38.6 degrees on our home thermometer, taken in the mouth about two hours ago; still burning up now
objective_data_offered.timing: about two hours ago and ongoing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single oral reading on a home thermometer; not clinically calibrated

objective_data_offered.value: He is shaking uncontrollably and his teeth are chattering — he can't hold still, and he is drenched in sweat under a pile of blankets
Blood is slowly oozing from his gums and he has had a nosebleed tonight, with no obvious cause
New little reddish-purple blood spots and bruises have appeared on both forearms
He goes very faint and lightheaded whenever he tries to sit up
Paracetamol 1000 mg two hours ago had zero effect on the fever
